rationale: The citing paper centrally analyzes fixed-fee vs pay-per-use/two-part contracts under behavioral distortions, and the cited paper is a major empirical study of flat-rate/pay-per-use tariff-choice biases, their causes, and profit consequences. Given this strong construct and setting overlap (tariff choice, overpayment bias, contract profitability), the citation is plausibly foundational or a direct comparison point rather than an obvious incidental mention.
